# Implementation Rules for AI Agents

This guide supplements `docs/extending.md` with agent-specific workflow
rules. **Read `docs/extending.md` first** — it is the authoritative
reference for how to implement filters, codecs, and pipeline nodes.

---

## Rule 1: Never Edit Generated Files

Generated files are produced by codegen during `cargo build`. If you
edit them directly, your changes will be silently overwritten.

**Generated files** (in `target/*/build/rasmcore-image-*/out/`):
- `generated_filter_adapter.rs`
- `generated_pipeline_nodes.rs`
- `generated_pipeline_adapter.rs`
- `generated_pipeline_mapper_adapter.rs`
- `generated_sdk_rust.rs`
- `generated_cli_dispatch.rs`
- `param-manifest.json`
- `param-manifest.hash`

**Also never edit:**
- `bindings.rs` (auto-generated by cargo-component for WASM builds)

**Instead:** Modify the source (registration macros, ConfigParams structs,
filter functions) and rebuild. See `docs/extending.md` for the full flow.

---

## Rule 2: Reference Validation Required

Every filter, codec, and transform must validate against an authoritative
reference implementation. No exceptions.

- **Cite the reference** in the `reference` attribute of `register_filter`
- **Write a parity test** comparing output to the reference
- **Document the alignment**: bit-exact, MAE < threshold, or PSNR > threshold
- **Document residuals** if output differs — explain exactly why

The only exception: algorithms that are explicitly designed to be
proprietary or novel (not based on any reference). Even then, document
the mathematical basis and test against known properties.

See `docs/extending.md#reference-validation` and `docs/REFERENCE_VALIDATION.md`.

---

## Rule 3: PRNG Only — No Non-Deterministic Randomness

Any algorithm with randomness MUST use `SmallRng::seed_from_u64(seed)`.

**Never use:** `OsRng`, `thread_rng()`, `StdRng::from_entropy()`, or
any non-deterministic source. Tests must be reproducible.

**Always expose** a `seed` parameter in the ConfigParams struct with
hint `rc.seed`.

---

## Rule 4: ConfigParams Required for All Parameterized Filters

Every filter with one or more parameters MUST use a ConfigParams struct.
No exceptions. Even single-parameter filters get a struct.

```rust
// CORRECT — always use a config struct
pub fn blur(pixels: &[u8], info: &ImageInfo, config: &BlurParams) -> Result<...>

// WRONG — never use bare parameters for registered filters
pub fn blur(pixels: &[u8], info: &ImageInfo, radius: f32) -> Result<...>
```

Zero-parameter filters (no params at all) do not need a struct.

The struct name MUST be `{PascalCaseFilterName}Params` for codegen to
auto-link it (e.g., filter `blur` → struct `BlurParams`).

---

## Rule 5: SIMD Required from Initial Implementation

All image processing operations must use SIMD intrinsics (SSE/NEON/WASM128)
from the initial implementation. SIMD is not deferred — it is part of the
first implementation.

See `rust.md` for SIMD guidelines.

---

## Rule 6: Zero-Warning Policy

All code must compile with zero warnings under `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`.
The validation script (`scripts/validate.sh`) enforces this.

---

## Rule 7: Point Ops Must Implement PointOp Trait

If a filter is a **per-channel mapping** (output channel depends only on
the corresponding input channel — brightness, contrast, gamma, levels,
posterize, solarize, invert, exposure, sigmoidal contrast), it MUST:

1. Implement `PointOp` trait on its ConfigParams struct (`build_lut()`)
2. Use `apply_lut(pixels, info, &config.build_lut())` as the function body

This enables automatic LUT fusion in the pipeline: consecutive point ops
compose into a single 256-entry table and execute in one memory pass.

SIMD can be used to accelerate LUT construction (computing the 256 values
in batches), but the LUT application (table lookup) is scalar.

Do NOT implement PointOp for multi-channel ops (hue rotation, channel
mixing, saturation) or position-dependent ops (vignette, gradient).

See `docs/extending.md` section "Implementing point ops with the PointOp
trait" for the full pattern.

---

## Rule 8: Validate Before Completion

Before marking any track complete, run the full validation:

```bash
./scripts/validate.sh
```

This checks: formatting (rustfmt), linting (clippy -D warnings), tests,
and build.

---

## Implementation Checklist

When implementing a new filter:

- [ ] ConfigParams struct with `#[derive(ConfigParams, Clone)]`
- [ ] `#[param(...)]` attributes on all fields (min, max, step, default)
- [ ] Filter function with `#[register_filter(...)]`
- [ ] `reference` attribute citing the reference implementation
- [ ] `overlap` attribute or appropriate config field for spatial filters
- [ ] `PointOp` trait impl if filter is a per-channel mapping (enables LUT fusion)
- [ ] Basic unit tests
- [ ] Parity test against reference implementation
- [ ] SIMD for spatial/color ops; SIMD optional for LUT construction in point ops
- [ ] `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes
- [ ] `cargo test` passes

When implementing a new codec:

- [ ] `#[register_decoder(...)]` or `#[register_encoder(...)]`
- [ ] Three-way validation (see `codec-validation.md`)
- [ ] Format coverage (bit depths, color modes, compression variants)
- [ ] Roundtrip test (encode → decode → compare)
- [ ] Reference comparison (see `encoder-params.md` for parameter mapping)
